title: ReDoS via user-supplied :regex patterns, no timeout
description: The :regex extension (RFC 5228 regex draft) accepts user-supplied patterns. fancy-regex falls back to backtracking for lookahead/lookbehind patterns. A pattern like '(?=(a+))+' against a long header value can cause catastrophic backtracking and hang evaluate() indefinitely. There is no timeout, no pattern complexity limit, and no documentation of this risk. Scripts compiled from untrusted sources (e.g., a web UI where users enter their own Sieve scripts) create a DoS surface. At minimum, document the risk; ideally add a pattern complexity check or use a linear-time regex crate for the :regex extension.
